channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump addresses nation on progress in Iran conflict, tensions remain high
item.description: On April 1, 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump addressed the nation regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran, asserting that American forces had achieved 'overwhelming victories' and that the core strate...

item.title: CCTV Reveals Final Movements of KMTC Student Before Her Death
item.description: In Karatina, Nyeri County, 20-year-old KMTC first-year student Sheila Chebet tragically lost her life, with investigations pointing to her boyfriend, Vincent Bett, as the main suspect. CCTV footage ca...

item.title: Conor D’Monte Receives 15-Year Sentence in B.C. Gang-Related Murder Conspiracy
item.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, Conor D’Monte has been sentenced to 15 years in prison following his conviction for conspiracy to commit murder, a sentence reflecting his involvement in ongoing gang c...

item.title: NASA Reveals Geological Origins of Mount Arkanu in the Sahara
item.description: NASA's Earth Observatory has uncovered the geological origins of Mount Arkanu, a massive formation in Libya's Sahara Desert previously thought to be caused by an extraterrestrial impact. The massif, n...

item.title: Government Directs Investigation into Alleged Fake Graduates at MMUST
item.description: The Kenyan government has directed the Commission for University Education (CUE) to investigate claims that Masinde Muliro University of Science and Technology (MMUST) awarded degrees to individuals w...

item.title: Trump Claims US Strategic Goals in Iran Close to Completion Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: United States President Donald Trump has stated that the US military's main strategic objectives in Iran are close to being achieved. In a televised address, he mentioned that the US intends to strike...

item.title: Police Arrest Alleged Drug Dealer and Confiscate R19 Million in Narcotics in KuGompo City
item.description: In a major intelligence-driven operation, police in KuGompo City apprehended a suspected drug lord believed to be orchestrating nationwide drug distribution. The operation resulted in the arrest of tw...

item.title: Filipinos rely on social media for medical guarantee letters amid hospital billing challenges
item.description: With rising hospital bills in the Philippines, many citizens are turning to Facebook groups for guidance on securing guarantee letters, official documents that promise payment for medical expenses whi...

item.title: Rain and Thunderstorms Expected Across the Philippines on Maundy Thursday
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has forecast that Easterlies will affect Metro Manila and other parts of the country on Maundy Thursday, April...

item.title: Three Plead Guilty in Saskatoon Murder Case of Jason Horse
item.description: The trial concerning the murder of Jason Horse in Saskatoon has concluded with all three accused entering guilty pleas. Robert Stone and Randy Brabant, who had previously pleaded not guilty to breakin...

item.title: Parliament approves Amaravati as the permanent capital of Andhra Pradesh
item.description: On Wednesday, the Lok Sabha passed the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation Amendment Bill, officially recognising Amaravati as the sole and permanent capital of Andhra Pradesh. The bill received support fro...

item.title: Single Mother Moves to Australia with Her Two Sons After 20 Years in Banking
item.description: Jesse Vutia, a single mother of two, has taken the bold step of resigning from her banking career of 20 years to move to Australia with her sons, Caleb, 12, and Caydon, 6. It will be their first time ...

item.title: Saskatchewan NDP Advocates for Enhanced Measures to Protect Against Gender-Based Violence
item.description: The Saskatchewan New Democratic Party (NDP) has publicly expressed deep concern over the ongoing issues of gender-based violence in the province, calling for stronger protective measures. Party repres...

item.title: Authorities seize 5.69 kilos of shabu at NAIA from international shipments
item.description: Authorities at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) intercepted illegal drugs worth P38.6 million on Tuesday. The seized contraband consisted of 5.69 kilos of shabu, hidden in three separate ...

item.title: The Rise of Olkaria: Kenya’s Geothermal Energy Success Story
item.description: Olkaria, located in Kenya’s Great Rift Valley, is now the heart of the country’s geothermal energy revolution. The journey started back in 1952 during British colonial rule when the first geothermal w...

item.title: Cubao Bus Terminal Crowded as Passengers Travel for Holy Week; NLEX Traffic Remains Light
item.description: Early on Maundy Thursday, many passengers heading to Bulacan and Nueva Ecija gathered at the bus terminal along EDSA in Cubao, Quezon City. Despite the terminal deploying 20 additional buses, a large ...

item.title: IDC Confirms Ongoing Support for Inclusive Industrial Growth and Black Entrepreneurship in South Africa
item.description: The Industrial Development Corporation (IDC) of South Africa has publicly reiterated its dedication to driving transformation, inclusive industrialisation, and support for black entrepreneurs. Amid re...

item.title: PCSO announces resumption of lotto sales and draws after Holy Week
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that lotto and other digit games will be temporarily suspended from today until Saturday in observance of Holy Week. According to a public ad...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions as Trump Signals Potential Military Action
item.description: Oil prices experienced significant volatility on Wednesday following statements by U.S. President Donald Trump regarding potential military action against Iran. Brent crude futures rose 5% to $106.42 ...

item.title: Higher Fuel and Energy Costs Set to Pressure South African Households and Agriculture
item.description: South African households are facing mounting financial pressure as fuel and electricity costs continue to climb. Petrol increased by R3.06 per litre, while diesel surged by R7.37 to R7.51 per litre. E...
